meta.title: CJK / Unicode Failure Corpus
meta.tagline: Real CJK, IME, and Unicode/text-handling bugs in open-source libraries. Repro, affected libs, and the fix.
meta.sourceNote: Most entries are pull requests authored by greymoth, taken verbatim from the GitHub API. A few are cited upstream issues from the wider ecosystem that document the same failures; those are marked cited and link to the original report.

ime-composition.label: IME composition
ime-composition.blurb: When a Japanese/Chinese/Korean user types, they press Enter to confirm an IME conversion (pick a kanji candidate). That same Enter often fires the component's own keydown / submit / select handler, so the form sends or an item is selected with half-finished text. The guard is one line: skip the handler while the composition is active (event.isComposing, or keyCode 229; in React read event.nativeEvent.isComposing).

kana-romaji.label: Kana / romaji
kana-romaji.blurb: Transliteration tables that drop or reverse kana. Round-trip is the oracle: kana to romaji and back should be stable, and a sibling (hiragana vs katakana) usually already does it right.

width-normalization.label: Width / normalization
width-normalization.blurb: Full-width to half-width, long-vowel marks, and kana range boundaries. Off-by-one range tables and missing digraphs silently corrupt text.

numeral.label: Numerals
numeral.blurb: Kanji numerals, including the daiji (traditional) forms used in legal and financial documents.

locale-leftover.label: Locale data
locale-leftover.blurb: Missing or wrong locale data: untranslated placeholders, mistranslations that flip meaning, labels left in English, and parse tables that drop a locale's diacritics so a formatted month will not parse back.

codegen-escape.label: Codegen escape
codegen-escape.blurb: Generators that interpolate text without escaping it. Special characters (including CJK and non-ASCII identifiers) leak into the output and break it.

regex-roundtrip.label: Regex roundtrip
regex-roundtrip.blurb: Parse to AST and back. A character that is special in one position (a leading caret, a hyphen) can be emitted unescaped and change what the pattern matches.

surrogate-emoji.label: Surrogate & grapheme
surrogate-emoji.blurb: Code that walks text by UTF-16 code unit or bare code point instead of by grapheme cluster. Surrogate pairs and non-BMP characters get split, ZWJ emoji and variation selectors are mis-detected, and combining marks or conjunct clusters drift away from their base.

segmentation.label: Segmentation / word count
segmentation.blurb: Word counts and reading-time estimates that split on spaces. CJK scripts put no spaces between words, so an entire Japanese or Chinese paragraph counts as one word: content gates reject valid answers and "min read" labels read as 1. The fix counts CJK characters separately from space-delimited words.

unicode-range.label: Unicode range
unicode-range.blurb: Character-class ranges that drift out of sync, so valid non-ASCII letters fall just outside the accepted set. An alpha rule accepts a letter that the matching alphanumeric rule rejects, and common accented characters fail validation even though their unaccented neighbours pass.

encoding.label: Encoding & BOM
encoding.blurb: Byte-order marks and charset edges. One code path strips a leading U+FEFF and a sibling does not, so the first field name or CSV header key arrives with an invisible BOM glued to it and lookups by that key silently miss.

entries.title: n-dynamic-tags commits a tag on the Enter that confirms an IME conversion
entries.symptom: Pressing Enter to confirm a kana to kanji conversion in an n-dynamic-tags input creates a tag from the in-progress text instead of just finishing the conversion.
entries.repro:
1. Render <n-dynamic-tags> and focus its input.
2. Switch to a Japanese IME, type "とうきょう", press Space to convert to 東京.
3. Press Enter to pick the candidate.
4. A tag is added from the unconfirmed text before the conversion commits.
entries.fix: Skip tag creation while e.isComposing is true; only act on the Enter that fires after compositionend.

entries.title: Chat box sends the message on the Enter that confirms an IME candidate
entries.symptom: Confirming a Japanese IME candidate with Enter in the chat box sends the message instead of committing the conversion.
entries.repro:
1. Open the chat input.
2. With a CJK IME, type a phrase and press Space to get conversion candidates.
3. Press Enter to choose a candidate.
4. The message sends with the unconfirmed text.
entries.fix: Return early when e.nativeEvent.isComposing is true. React's synthetic KeyboardEvent has no isComposing field, so read it off nativeEvent (the DOM event), not e.isComposing.

entries.title: Command palette runs the highlighted command on the IME confirm Enter
entries.symptom: Typing a CJK query in the command palette and pressing Enter to confirm the IME runs the highlighted command instead of finishing the conversion.
entries.repro:
1. Open the command palette.
2. Compose a Japanese search term with the IME.
3. Press Enter to commit the conversion.
4. The first/highlighted command fires prematurely.
entries.fix: Add `if (e.isComposing) return` at the top of the palette input keydown handler. Svelte exposes the native KeyboardEvent, so e.isComposing works directly.

entries.title: Confirming a CJK @-mention with Enter replaces it with the highlighted option (Safari)
entries.symptom: On Safari/WebKit, composing an @-mention name in CJK and pressing Enter to confirm the IME replaces the text with whatever mention is highlighted.
entries.repro:
1. In Safari, type @ then compose a Japanese name with the IME.
2. Press Enter to confirm the kanji conversion.
3. The composing text is replaced by the highlighted mention option.
entries.fix: In the ENTER branch, return early on event.nativeEvent.isComposing before preventDefault. WebKit reports the commit keydown as which===Enter + isComposing:true; Chromium reports keyCode 229 and never enters this branch.

entries.title: Enter that confirms an IME composition also selects the active option
entries.symptom: The Enter used to commit an IME composition also selects the currently highlighted option in a searchable Select.
entries.repro:
1. Open a searchable <Select>.
2. Type a CJK query with the IME and press Enter to confirm the conversion.
3. The active option is selected with the unfinished query.
entries.fix: Track composition state (compositionstart/compositionend) and skip option selection while composing.

entries.title: time-picker handles keys during IME composition
entries.symptom: The time-picker's key handler fires while an IME composition is active, so keystrokes meant for the IME mutate the time value.
entries.repro:
1. Focus the time-picker input.
2. Begin an IME composition.
3. Composition keystrokes are intercepted by the picker's key handler instead of the IME.
entries.fix: Skip the picker's keydown handling while the composition is active (isComposing guard).

entries.title: VAutocomplete keydown listener fires during IME composition
entries.symptom: VAutocomplete's keydown listener triggers during IME composition, so confirming a CJK query navigates or selects options.
entries.repro:
1. Open a VAutocomplete.
2. Compose a Japanese query, press Enter/arrow to confirm.
3. The keydown listener acts on the composing keys.
entries.fix: Bail out of the keydown listener when e.isComposing is true.

entries.title: defineShortcuts fires global shortcuts while typing with an IME
entries.symptom: Single-key shortcuts registered with defineShortcuts fire while composing text, so romaji keystrokes trigger app shortcuts mid-composition.
entries.repro:
1. Register a single-key shortcut (e.g. 'g').
2. In an input, compose Japanese text whose romaji includes that key.
3. The shortcut fires during composition.
entries.fix: Ignore key events when e.isComposing (or keyCode 229) before dispatching shortcuts.

entries.title: color-picker channel input handles Enter during IME composition
entries.symptom: A color-picker channel input runs its Enter handler while an IME composition is active, committing before the composition ends.
entries.repro:
1. Focus a color channel input.
2. Trigger an IME composition and press Enter.
3. The channel commit fires before compositionend.
entries.fix: Guard the channel input's Enter handler with isComposing.

entries.title: Inline file rename submits while the IME is composing
entries.symptom: Renaming a file inline and pressing Enter to confirm a CJK name submits the rename mid-composition.
entries.repro:
1. Start an inline file rename.
2. Compose a Japanese filename with the IME.
3. Press Enter to confirm the conversion; the rename submits with the unfinished name.
entries.fix: Skip the rename submit while the IME is composing (isComposing guard).

entries.title: Angular chat input submits on the IME confirm Enter
entries.symptom: The Angular chat input submits the message on the Enter that confirms an IME composition.
entries.repro:
1. Type a CJK message in the Angular chat input.
2. Press Enter to confirm the IME conversion; the message sends early.
entries.fix: Check event.isComposing (Angular passes the native KeyboardEvent) before submitting.

entries.title: Composer sends on the Enter that confirms an IME composition
entries.symptom: The Composer sends the message on the Enter used to confirm an IME composition.
entries.repro:
1. Compose CJK text in the Composer.
2. Press Enter to pick a candidate; the message sends instead of committing.
entries.fix: Skip send when e.nativeEvent.isComposing is true.

entries.title: Katakana ン loses the syllabic-n apostrophe before vowels and Y
entries.symptom: Katakana ン before a vowel or Y is romanized without the apostrophe, unlike hiragana ん. シンヨウ becomes SHINYOU (should be SHIN'YOU), so it collides with シニョウ.
entries.fix: Map katakana ン to N' (matching hiragana) and add the ンー long-vowel digraph as N', so N is never a katakana map key and toKatakana still round-trips (PAN to パン).

entries.title: Missing っでぃ (ddi) sokuon entry for Hepburn and Kunrei
entries.symptom: The geminated d + small i sequence っでぃ (ddi) has no Hepburn or Kunrei entry, so loanword spellings that use it romanize incorrectly.
entries.repro: Convert a word containing っでぃ; the doubled d before the small i is not produced.
entries.fix: Add the missing っでぃ (ddi) sokuon entry to both the Hepburn and Kunrei tables.

entries.title: Historical kana ゐ/ゑ missing from the hiragana/katakana mapping
entries.symptom: The historical kana ゐ (wi) and ゑ (we) are absent from the mapping, so text containing them is dropped or left unconverted.
entries.repro: Convert text containing ゐ or ゑ (or katakana ヰ/ヱ); the characters pass through unmapped.
entries.fix: Add ゐ/ゑ and their katakana counterparts to the kana mapping.

entries.title: kana2alphabet does not romanize small katakana ヵ/ヶ
entries.symptom: kana2alphabet does not handle the small katakana ヵ/ヶ (small ka/ke), so counters like 一ヶ月 are mis-romanized.
entries.repro:
import jaconv
jaconv.kana2alphabet('ヶ')  # small ke not handled
entries.fix: Add the small ka/ke mappings to kana2alphabet.

entries.title: Reversed ヲ/ヺ voiced-mark mapping in addVoicedMarks and combineSoundMarks
entries.symptom: The ヲ to ヺ voiced-mark mapping is reversed, so adding or stripping a dakuten on ヲ produces the wrong character.
entries.repro: Add a voiced mark to ヲ; expected ヺ, got the wrong glyph because the mapping is reversed.
entries.fix: Correct the reversed ヲ/ヺ entry in both addVoicedMarks and combineSoundMarks.

entries.title: ー (long-vowel mark) expands incorrectly after katakana ヒ and ビ
entries.symptom: During normalization, the chōonpu (ー) is expanded with the wrong vowel after katakana ヒ and ビ.
entries.repro: Normalize ヒー / ビー; the long-vowel expansion produces the wrong vowel for these two kana.
entries.fix: Fix the ー-expansion table entries for ヒ and ビ.

entries.title: Width/kana conversion drops the first and last character of each range
entries.symptom: Full-width/half-width and kana range conversions skip the boundary characters of each range (！ ～ ぁ ゖ ...), so edge code points like ！ (U+FF01) are not converted.
entries.repro: moji('！～').convert('ZE', 'HE').toString()  // boundary chars at range edges are skipped
entries.fix: Convert the first and last character of each range, not just the interior.

entries.title: kanji2number cannot parse 萬 (daiji form of 万)
entries.symptom: kanji2number cannot parse 萬, the daiji (大字) traditional form of 万 (10,000), so legal and financial documents that use 大字 numerals fail to convert.
entries.repro:
from kanjize import kanji2number
kanji2number('萬')  # not recognized as 10000
entries.fix: Map 萬 to 万 (10,000) in kanji2number.

entries.title: Wrong ja-JP Typography expand/collapse labels
entries.symptom: The ja-JP labels for Typography's expand/collapse control were incorrect, so Japanese users saw the wrong 展開/折りたたみ text.
entries.repro:
1. Set ConfigProvider locale to ja_JP.
2. Render <Typography.Paragraph ellipsis={{ expandable: true }}>.
3. The expand/collapse label is wrong.
entries.fix: Correct the ja-JP expand/collapse label strings.

entries.title: weekPlaceholder left untranslated in the Japanese locale
entries.symptom: weekPlaceholder was missing from the Japanese locale, so the week date-picker showed an English placeholder.
entries.repro: Use the date picker in week mode with the ja locale; the placeholder renders in English.
entries.fix: Add the Japanese weekPlaceholder translation.

entries.title: Japanese future times used 以内 (within) instead of 後 (later)
entries.symptom: Future timestamps in the ja locale used 以内 (within) instead of 後 (later), so '3 minutes from now' rendered as 3分以内 (within 3 minutes), which means the opposite.
entries.repro: timeago a future timestamp with the ja locale; it renders '3分以内' instead of '3分後'.
entries.fix: Use 後 for future time strings in the ja locale.

entries.title: Japanese day-of-month step description not scoped to a month
entries.symptom: The Japanese description for a day-of-month step was not scoped to a month, producing a mistranslated cron description.
entries.repro: cronstrue.toString('0 0 1-31/3 * *', { locale: 'ja' }); the description is not scoped to the month.
entries.fix: Scope the ja day-of-month step description to a month.

entries.title: Generator emits a leading ^ unescaped, inverting [a^] into [^a]
entries.symptom: Optimizing or regenerating a character class can move a literal ^ to the front and emit it unescaped, flipping the meaning: [a^] round-trips to [^a] (a negated class).
entries.repro:
const rt = require('regexp-tree')
rt.optimize('/[a^]/').toString() // '/[^a]/'  <- now matches the negation
entries.fix: In the generator, escape a leading literal ^ in a non-negative character class so generate(parse(x)) preserves meaning.

entries.title: Enum member names with special characters produce invalid TypeScript
entries.symptom: Enum member names containing special characters (including non-ASCII / CJK) are emitted unescaped, so the generated enum does not compile.
entries.repro: Generate types from a schema whose enum values contain quotes or special characters; the emitted enum has invalid member identifiers and tsc errors.
entries.fix: Escape enum member names so special characters produce valid output.

entries.title: Formatter over-escapes a mid-line # (C# becomes C\#)
entries.symptom: The formatter over-escapes a # in the middle of a line because the heading branch of the escape regex is not anchored to line start: 'C# is a language' becomes 'C\# is a language'.
entries.repro: format(parse('C# is a language')) // 'C\# is a language' - the '#' is escaped mid-sentence
entries.fix: Anchor the heading alternative: change #+\s to ^#+\s, mirroring the already-anchored ^* and ^> cases.

entries.title: Guard IME composition in the agent session rename input
entries.symptom: Pressing Enter to confirm CJK composition in the agent session rename input also triggers submit/rename, discarding the composed text or submitting prematurely.
entries.repro: Use Japanese/Chinese/Korean IME in siyuan's session rename field; press Enter to confirm composition; rename fires before composition is complete.
entries.fix: Check event.isComposing (and event.keyCode===229 for legacy browsers) before acting on keydown/keyup Enter. Ignore Enter events during composition.

entries.title: fix: ignore Enter that confirms IME composition in chat input
entries.symptom: CJK users pressing Enter to accept an IME candidate also submits the chat message, sending an empty or partial message.
entries.repro: Type Japanese in llmchat chat input using IME; press Enter to select a candidate; message sends immediately.
entries.fix: Guard Enter keydown handler with if (event.isComposing || event.nativeEvent?.isComposing) return.

entries.title: fix: ignore Enter that confirms IME composition in chat input
entries.symptom: Confirming CJK IME candidate with Enter in AIaW chat input simultaneously submits the message.
entries.repro: Japanese IME → type → Enter to confirm → message sent before input complete.
entries.fix: Ignore Enter keydown when isComposing is true.

entries.title: fix: ignore Enter during IME composition in chat input
entries.symptom: Obsidian Smart Composer chat submits messages on IME Enter key in macOS/Windows with CJK input methods.
entries.repro: Use Japanese IME in Obsidian Smart Composer chat; confirm composition with Enter; chat sends.
entries.fix: Check isComposing flag before processing Enter keydown.

entries.title: fix(web): ignore IME composition Enter in ListFieldInput
entries.symptom: ListFieldInput in Onyx web accepts a list entry on the IME confirmation Enter, adding an empty or partial item.
entries.repro: ListFieldInput with CJK IME → Enter to confirm → list item added prematurely.
entries.fix: Skip Enter action when event.nativeEvent.isComposing is true.

entries.title: fix(InputPicker): ignore Enter during IME composition when selecting an item
entries.symptom: RSuite InputPicker selects the focused item when CJK IME confirmation Enter is pressed, skipping intended input.
entries.repro: RSuite InputPicker with CJK IME → search → Enter to confirm composition → list item selected prematurely.
entries.fix: Guard item selection Enter handler with isComposing check.

entries.title: fix(web): guard IME composition in tag input Enter handler
entries.symptom: Dify tag input adds a tag on IME confirmation Enter, creating malformed tags for CJK users.
entries.repro: Dify web tag input → Japanese IME → Enter to confirm → tag added prematurely.
entries.fix: Check isComposing before handling Enter in tag input.

entries.title: fix: ignore Enter during IME composition in remaining text inputs
entries.symptom: Multiple Flowise text inputs fire on IME Enter, interrupting CJK text composition.
entries.repro: Flowise text inputs with CJK IME → Enter to confirm → premature action fired.
entries.fix: Add isComposing guard to all remaining Enter keydown handlers.

entries.title: fix(frontend): guard IME composition in inline rename Enter handlers
entries.symptom: Langflow inline rename inputs commit the rename on IME Enter, saving partial CJK names.
entries.repro: Langflow node rename → CJK IME → Enter to confirm composition → rename saved with partial text.
entries.fix: Guard Enter handler in inline rename with event.nativeEvent?.isComposing check.

entries.title: fix(ui): ignore Enter during IME composition in select dropdowns
entries.symptom: Plane select dropdowns pick an item on IME confirmation Enter, making it impossible for CJK users to search.
entries.repro: Plane dropdown search → Japanese IME → Enter to confirm → item picked mid-composition.
entries.fix: Skip item selection when event.nativeEvent.isComposing is true.

entries.title: fix: ignore IME composition Enter in chat-thread and attachment rename inputs
entries.symptom: Twenty CRM chat-thread send and attachment rename both fire on IME Enter, breaking CJK workflows.
entries.repro: Twenty CRM chat or attachment rename → CJK IME → Enter to confirm → send/rename triggered.
entries.fix: Check event.nativeEvent?.isComposing before handling Enter.

entries.title: fix(editor): ignore IME composition keys in search navigation
entries.symptom: Excalidraw search navigation (arrow keys, Enter) fires during CJK IME composition, moving selection mid-composition.
entries.repro: Excalidraw search → CJK IME → type → Arrow/Enter during composition → result navigation fires.
entries.fix: Guard navigation key handlers with event.isComposing check.

entries.title: fix(ui): ignore the IME composition Enter in SearchInput
entries.symptom: Payload CMS SearchInput submits on IME confirmation Enter, bypassing the intended search query.
entries.repro: Payload admin search → CJK IME → Enter to confirm → search fires with partial query.
entries.fix: Ignore Enter in SearchInput when event.nativeEvent?.isComposing is true.

entries.title: fix(board): ignore IME composition Enter when editing card and column titles
entries.symptom: Trilium kanban card and column title editing commits on IME Enter, saving partial CJK text.
entries.repro: Trilium board → edit card title → CJK IME → Enter to confirm → title saved with partial text.
entries.fix: Check isComposing before committing title edits on Enter.

entries.title: Diagrams: skip IME composition Enter in custom instruction
entries.symptom: big-AGI custom instruction field fires on IME Enter, submitting partial CJK instructions.
entries.repro: big-AGI custom instruction → CJK IME → Enter to confirm → instruction submitted prematurely.
entries.fix: Skip Enter action when event.nativeEvent?.isComposing is true.

entries.title: fix: guard IME composition on Enter in prompt name/labels and dynamic tag inputs
entries.symptom: LibreChat prompt name, label, and tag inputs all trigger on IME Enter, causing broken CJK input across multiple components.
entries.repro: LibreChat prompt editor → tag or name field → CJK IME → Enter to confirm → submit fires.
entries.fix: isComposing guard across prompt name, label, and dynamic-tag Enter handlers.

entries.title: fix: ignore IME composition Enter in rename and project dialogs
entries.symptom: Jan AI assistant rename and project dialogs accept partial CJK names when Enter confirms IME composition.
entries.repro: Jan rename dialog → CJK IME → Enter to confirm → dialog committed with partial name.
entries.fix: Guard Enter submit with event.nativeEvent?.isComposing check.

entries.title: Romanize づ as zu (the consonant was being dropped)
entries.symptom: kana-romaji library drops the 'z' consonant when romanizing づ, outputting 'u' instead of 'zu'.
entries.repro: romanize('づ') returns 'u' instead of 'zu'.
entries.fix: づ should romanize as 'zu' (Hepburn). Map づ→zu in the kana table.

entries.title: Fix script detection of halfwidth katakana and fullwidth forms
entries.symptom: Meilisearch's charabia tokenizer incorrectly classifies halfwidth katakana (U+FF65-U+FF9F) and some fullwidth forms as wrong scripts, causing them to be processed by the wrong tokenizer and failing Japanese search.
entries.repro: Index halfwidth katakana text in Meilisearch; search for the same terms; results missing because script detection classifies halfwidth katakana as non-Japanese.
entries.fix: Extend script detection to recognize U+FF65–U+FF9F (halfwidth katakana) as Japanese script.

entries.title: Keep combining marks with their base grapheme in Width::wrap
entries.symptom: tabled's text-wrapping splits combining marks (diacritics, dakuten, etc.) away from their base grapheme when calculating width for terminal table cells.
entries.repro: Create a tabled table with text containing combining marks (e.g., 'が' as 'か' + combining voiced mark U+3099); wrapping separates the base and combining mark onto different lines.
entries.fix: Use a grapheme cluster iterator (Unicode UAX #29) when wrapping, never splitting within a grapheme cluster.

entries.title: editor: Center the block cursor glyph for ambiguous-width characters
entries.symptom: In Zed editor, the block cursor over Unicode ambiguous-width characters (East Asian Width 'A' category, e.g., some symbols, box-drawing) is misaligned — the cursor glyph is not centered in the cell.
entries.repro: Open Zed with a CJK font; position cursor on an ambiguous-width character; block cursor appears shifted or misaligned.
entries.fix: Center the block cursor glyph using the rendered cell width rather than the glyph's intrinsic width for ambiguous-width characters.

entries.title: fix: avoid splitting surrogate pairs when truncating wide characters
entries.symptom: cli-table3 truncates text by byte/code-unit count rather than code-point count, splitting surrogate pairs in emoji or supplementary CJK characters, producing mojibake in terminal table cells.
entries.repro: Create a cli-table3 table with a column containing emoji (e.g., 🎉) or supplementary CJK characters; set a column width that truncates mid-emoji; output shows garbled characters.
entries.fix: Use a Unicode-aware splitter (spread operator or Array.from) to iterate code points rather than code units when truncating.

entries.title: fix(ui): keep truncateWithEndVisible code-point-safe in short-width fallback
entries.symptom: Clerk UI's truncateWithEndVisible function uses substring/slice on raw code units in its short-width fallback, splitting surrogate pairs in emoji or non-BMP characters.
entries.repro: A Clerk UI component displaying an email/name containing emoji in the short-width fallback path; the truncated string ends mid-surrogate-pair, showing '?' or garbled chars.
entries.fix: Use Array.from() or spread to split by code points, or use Intl.Segmenter, before truncating.

entries.title: fix(cmap): clamp format 12/13 character codes to the Unicode maximum
entries.symptom: opentype.js does not clamp cmap format 12/13 character codes to U+10FFFF; malformed fonts with out-of-range codes cause incorrect glyph lookups for supplementary characters.
entries.repro: Load a font with a cmap subtable containing entries beyond U+10FFFF; glyph lookup for supplementary characters (emoji, CJK Extension B+) returns wrong glyph.
entries.fix: Clamp all format 12/13 startCharCode/endCharCode values to 0x10FFFF during parsing.

entries.title: fix: handle non-BMP punctuation and symbols in smart quotes
entries.symptom: markdown-it's smart quotes replacement does not handle non-BMP punctuation and symbols (U+10000+); surrounding text with supplementary characters causes wrong quote pairing or no conversion.
entries.repro: markdown-it smartquotes on text adjacent to emoji or supplementary Unicode symbols (e.g., '𝕳ello'); smart quote pairing is incorrect.
entries.fix: Use a regex that is Unicode-aware for the 'whitespace' and 'punctuation' character class checks, or use Array.from for code-point iteration.

entries.title: Treat Indic conjunct clusters as a single grapheme (UAX #29 GB9c)
entries.symptom: Slate rich-text editor does not implement Unicode UAX #29 GB9c rule, splitting Indic conjunct clusters (consonant + virama + consonant sequences) across grapheme boundaries, causing incorrect cursor positioning and deletion in Hindi, Bengali, Tamil, etc.
entries.repro: Type a conjunct consonant in Hindi (e.g., 'क्ष') in Slate; press Backspace; only one codepoint is deleted instead of the full cluster.
entries.fix: Apply Unicode GB9c rule: treat <Indic_Conjunct_Break=Linker> sequences as a single grapheme cluster.

entries.title: Fix emphasis flanking for Unicode marks and format characters
entries.symptom: wenmode's is_punctuation function treats Unicode combining marks (Mn category) and format characters (Cf/ZWJ) as punctuation per the CommonMark spec, incorrectly suppressing valid emphasis around CJK text with diacritics or ZWJ sequences.
entries.repro: Markdown text using emphasis (* or _) adjacent to a combining mark or ZWJ character; emphasis fails to render because combining marks are classified as punctuation.
entries.fix: Exclude Unicode General Categories Mn, Mc, Cf from the punctuation classification; add ASCII fast-path to avoid performance regression.

entries.title: fix(@formatjs/intl-relativetimeformat): honor numberingSystem option
entries.symptom: formatjs intl-relativetimeformat ignores the numberingSystem locale option (e.g., 'jpan', 'arab'), always producing Latin numerals in relative time strings.
entries.repro: new Intl.RelativeTimeFormat('ja-JP-u-nu-jpan', {}).format(-1, 'day') returns '1日前' with Latin '1' instead of Japanese numeral '一日前'.
entries.fix: Apply the numberingSystem extension from the locale tag when formatting relative time numbers.

entries.title: Fix ja aria filterConstraint translation (成約 → 制約)
entries.symptom: PrimeLocale Japanese (ja) locale uses 成約 (meaning 'conclusion of a contract') for 'filterConstraint' aria label, which should be 制約 ('constraint/restriction').
entries.repro: PrimeFaces component with aria-label for filter constraint in Japanese reads '成約' (contract) instead of '制約' (constraint).
entries.fix: Change filterConstraint value from '成約' to '制約' in the ja.json locale file.

entries.title: fix(locale): correct Japanese (ja-JP) translations
entries.symptom: Vant component library Japanese (ja-JP) locale contains incorrect translations for multiple UI strings.
entries.repro: Vant components with ja-JP locale display mistranslated strings for picker, datetime, and other components.
entries.fix: Correct multiple mistranslated strings in vant/src/locale/lang/ja-JP.ts.

entries.title: [l10n] Improve Japanese (ja-JP) locale
entries.symptom: MUI X Japanese (ja-JP) locale has incomplete or incorrect translations for data grid, date picker, and time picker components.
entries.repro: MUI X components with jaJP locale show untranslated English strings or incorrect Japanese.
entries.fix: Improve Japanese translations across multiple MUI X locale strings.

entries.title: fix(locale): add missing Japanese Upload crop modal text
entries.symptom: Semi Design's Japanese locale is missing translation strings for the Upload component's crop modal UI.
entries.repro: Semi Design Upload component with crop modal in Japanese locale shows untranslated (English) strings in the crop modal.
entries.fix: Add missing Japanese translation keys for Upload crop modal to ja-JP locale.

entries.title: fix(module:i18n): add missing quarter placeholders to ja_JP
entries.symptom: NG-ZORRO Angular Japanese (ja_JP) locale is missing quarter placeholder strings, causing runtime errors or blank UI for date range pickers.
entries.repro: NG-ZORRO DatePicker quarter mode with ja_JP locale throws error or shows blank quarter placeholders.
entries.fix: Add missing quarter i18n keys to ja_JP locale file.

entries.title: Fix ja-ja labelFileProcessing (読込中 -> アップロード中)
entries.symptom: FilePond Japanese locale uses '読込中' (loading/reading) for the file processing label, which should be 'アップロード中' (uploading) to accurately describe the action.
entries.repro: FilePond with Japanese locale shows '読込中' during file upload, misleading users into thinking a read is occurring.
entries.fix: Change labelFileProcessing from '読込中' to 'アップロード中' in the Japanese locale.

entries.title: fix(locale): correct ja-JP translation errors
entries.symptom: Arco Design Japanese (ja-JP) locale contains multiple translation errors across component strings.
entries.repro: Arco Design components with ja-JP locale show incorrect Japanese translations.
entries.fix: Correct translation errors in arco-design/arco-design/components/_utils/locale/ja-JP.ts.

entries.title: fix(i18n): complete Japanese (ja) translation
entries.symptom: jsoneditor's Japanese locale is incomplete; many UI strings remain in English when ja locale is selected.
entries.repro: jsoneditor with locale:'ja' shows English strings for missing Japanese translations.
entries.fix: Fill all missing Japanese translation keys in the ja locale file.

entries.title: fix(lang): correct Japanese (ja) mistranslations in editor/tree labels
entries.symptom: Quasar Framework Japanese locale has mistranslations in editor toolbar labels and tree component strings.
entries.repro: Quasar editor toolbar or tree component in Japanese shows incorrect label text.
entries.fix: Correct mistranslated Japanese strings in quasar/lang/ja.js.

entries.title: fix(@uppy/locales): repair smart_count placeholder in ja_JP folderAdded
entries.symptom: Uppy's Japanese (ja_JP) locale has a broken smart_count plural placeholder in the 'folderAdded' string, causing pluralization to fail and show a raw placeholder.
entries.repro: Uppy file picker in Japanese displays 'folderAdded' with a visible placeholder token instead of correct plural text.
entries.fix: Fix the smart_count placeholder syntax in ja_JP's folderAdded locale string.

entries.title: fix(lang): add missing Japanese (ja) translation for 'Playing in Picture-in-Picture'
entries.symptom: Video.js Japanese (ja) locale is missing the translation for the 'Playing in Picture-in-Picture' accessibility string, falling back to English.
entries.repro: Video.js in Japanese locale; PiP mode accessible label shows 'Playing in Picture-in-Picture' in English.
entries.fix: Add 'ピクチャーインピクチャーで再生中' or equivalent to the ja.json locale.

entries.title: i18n(ja): complete Japanese translations — fill 24 missing keys
entries.symptom: Memos Japanese (ja) locale has 24 missing translation keys; these fall back to English in the UI.
entries.repro: Memos with Japanese locale selected; 24 UI strings display in English.
entries.fix: Fill all 24 missing Japanese translation keys in locales/ja.json.

entries.title: i18n(ja): fill 48 missing email translation keys
entries.symptom: Appwrite Japanese email templates have 48 missing translation keys, causing emails to be sent in English to Japanese users.
entries.repro: Appwrite with Japanese locale; email notifications contain 48 English fallback strings.
entries.fix: Translate all 48 missing keys in app/config/locale/translations/ja.json email templates.

entries.title: i18n(ja): complete dashboard translations — fill 511 missing keys
entries.symptom: Medusa dashboard Japanese (ja) locale has 511 missing translation keys, leaving most of the admin UI in English for Japanese users.
entries.repro: Medusa admin dashboard with Japanese locale; majority of UI strings display in English.
entries.fix: Translate all 511 missing keys in packages/admin-ui/ui/src/i18n/translations/ja.json.

entries.title: feat(localizations): translate oauthConsent section to Japanese
entries.symptom: Clerk's oauthConsent UI section lacks Japanese translations, showing English strings to Japanese users during OAuth authorization.
entries.repro: Clerk OAuth consent page with Japanese locale; consent section displays in English.
entries.fix: Add Japanese translations for all oauthConsent locale keys.

entries.title: fix: correct enName of 愛知県 (Aichi) from 'ehime' to 'aichi'
entries.symptom: jp-prefectures.js has the English name of Aichi prefecture (愛知県) set to 'ehime' (which is Ehime prefecture / 愛媛県), causing incorrect prefecture mapping.
entries.repro: jpPrefectures.findByCode(23).enName returns 'ehime' instead of 'aichi'.
entries.fix: Change enName for 愛知県 (code 23) from 'ehime' to 'aichi'.

entries.title: onChange fires during IME composition in controlled components
entries.symptom: In a controlled <input>/<textarea>, onChange fires for the intermediate keystrokes of an IME composition, so any onChange-driven search or filter runs on half-finished CJK text.
entries.repro: Type Chinese/Japanese through an IME into a controlled input that filters on onChange; the handler runs on every uncommitted composition keystroke.
entries.fix: Track compositionstart/compositionend and suppress onChange handling while a composition is active.

entries.title: Entry.Completed fires on the Enter that confirms an IME candidate
entries.symptom: On Windows, Entry.Completed is raised when the user presses Enter inside the IME conversion candidate window, so completion fires before the CJK conversion is committed.
entries.repro: Focus a MAUI Entry on Windows, use a Chinese/Japanese IME, press Enter to pick a candidate; Completed fires mid-conversion.
entries.fix: Do not raise Completed while the IME conversion window is open; fire only once the composition is fully committed.

entries.title: Event order between compositionend and input is inconsistent across browsers
entries.symptom: The spec orders input before compositionend (Chrome and Safari follow it), but Firefox and Edge fire input after compositionend, so isComposing-based guards behave differently per browser.
entries.repro: Log the input and compositionend events while committing a CJK composition in Chrome versus Firefox; their relative order differs.
entries.fix: Define a canonical order in the spec; frameworks should not assume input fires before compositionend.

entries.title: insertCompositionText and isComposing cannot single out the commit input event
entries.symptom: Every input event during a composition, including the one that commits it on Enter, carries isComposing=true and inputType insertCompositionText, so code cannot detect the commit without also listening for compositionend.
entries.repro: Type s, i, Space to convert to a kanji, change the candidate, then press Enter to commit; the input events for each step are indistinguishable.
entries.fix: Assign a distinct inputType (or property) to the input event that commits the composition.

entries.title: Vim jk escape depends on the CJK IME input mode
entries.symptom: With Vim mode and a CJK IME, the jk insert-mode escape only fires while the IME is composing in Chinese mode; in direct English input it inserts a literal j and k instead of escaping.
entries.repro: Enable Vim mode, map jk to escape, use a CJK IME such as macOS Pinyin; jk escapes while composing in Chinese mode but not in English mode.
entries.fix: Detect the jk key sequence consistently regardless of the IME composition state.

entries.title: Text shifts vertically while composing with a Chinese IME on Windows
entries.symptom: While composing Chinese text with an IME on Windows, the line of text jumps vertically as the composition updates, which disrupts reading and editing.
entries.repro: Open Zed (Vim mode) on Windows 11 and type Chinese characters through an IME; the text shifts vertically during composition.
entries.fix: Keep the line baseline stable while updating the IME marked-text region during composition.

entries.title: Marked text (IME preedit) is not rendered, so composition is blind
entries.symptom: Warp does not render IME marked (preedit) text, so dead keys and CJK composition show nothing until committed and the user composes blind.
entries.repro: In Warp on macOS, press Option+E (or start a CJK composition); no marked text is shown to indicate the in-progress input.
entries.fix: Render marked text inline via the platform IME API (NSTextInputClient on macOS) so the preedit is visible at the cursor.

entries.title: ZWJ-joined emoji are split into pieces
entries.symptom: grapheme-splitter breaks ZWJ-joined emoji into parts instead of one grapheme cluster: the rainbow flag splits into its component glyphs, and skin-tone sequences come apart.
entries.repro: new GraphemeSplitter().splitGraphemes('🏳️‍🌈') returns two elements instead of one.
entries.fix: Implement the Unicode emoji ZWJ sequence rules (UTS #51) so a ZWJ-joined emoji stays a single cluster.

entries.title: toArray splits a tag-sequence flag emoji into code points
entries.symptom: _.toArray splits an emoji built from a tag sequence (a subdivision flag) into its component code points instead of returning it as one element.
entries.repro: _.toArray for the England flag emoji (a base flag plus tag characters) returns seven pieces instead of one.
entries.fix: Use a Unicode-aware iterator such as Intl.Segmenter that handles tag sequences when converting a string to an array.

entries.title: Half-width katakana fails to romanize, especially with voiced marks
entries.symptom: pykakasi does not romanize half-width katakana correctly, particularly when a half-width voiced or semi-voiced mark (U+FF9E / U+FF9F) follows the base kana.
entries.repro: Convert half-width katakana such as a half-width ka followed by a half-width dakuten; the output is wrong instead of 'ga'.
entries.fix: NFKC-normalize half-width katakana and combining voiced marks to their full-width equivalents before romanization.

entries.title: Faulty transliteration of half-width katakana with dakuten and handakuten
entries.symptom: Python unidecode transliterates half-width katakana carrying dakuten/handakuten incorrectly, producing artifacts, while hiragana and full-width katakana romanize correctly.
entries.repro: unidecode on half-width ba bi bu be bo returns a wrong string instead of 'babibubebo'.
entries.fix: Pre-compose half-width katakana plus combining voiced marks (NFKC) before the transliteration lookup.

entries.title: Variation Selector-15 (U+FE0E) is ignored, forcing emoji style
entries.symptom: Windows Terminal ignores the text-presentation variation selector U+FE0E, rendering the color-emoji form even when text style is explicitly requested.
entries.repro: Print a text-style sequence such as U+23CF followed by U+FE0E; it renders as a color emoji instead of the text glyph.
entries.fix: Honor VS-15 (U+FE0E) for text presentation and VS-16 (U+FE0F) for emoji presentation, per the Unicode emoji variation sequences.

entries.title: Matches a character followed by a text variation selector (U+FE0E)
entries.symptom: emoji-regex matches a base character even when it is followed by U+FE0E (the text variation selector), so text-presentation characters are wrongly classified as emoji.
entries.repro: emojiRegex().test('\u2757\uFE0E') returns true even though U+FE0E requests text presentation.
entries.fix: Exclude a match followed by VS-15 (U+FE0E); treat only a trailing VS-16 (U+FE0F) or no selector as emoji.

entries.title: Onboarding answer gate counts a spaceless CJK answer as one word
entries.symptom: Onboarding decides whether a spoken answer has enough content with len(transcript.split()) >= 2. str.split() returns 1 for CJK text that has no spaces, so a full answer like 東京に住んでいます is counted as a single word, never reaches the LLM check, and the question stays marked unanswered for Japanese, Chinese, and Korean speakers.
entries.repro: len('東京に住んでいます'.split())  # 1, so word_count >= 2 is False and the answer is rejected
entries.fix: Use the existing CJK-aware _word_count helper (already used by should_discard_conversation) instead of plain split(); it falls back to split() for non-CJK text, so English input is unchanged.

entries.title: Editor footer treats a whole CJK paragraph as one word and one minute read
entries.symptom: The editor footer's word count and reading time come from TipTap's CharacterCount, whose default wordCounter splits on spaces. CJK scripts have no spaces between words, so a long Japanese or Chinese draft shows "1 word" and "1 min read" even though the published page, which already counts CJK separately, reports the correct time.
entries.repro: Type a CJK paragraph into the editor; CharacterCount's default text.split(' ') returns 1, so the footer reports 1 word and 1 min read.
entries.fix: Configure CharacterCount with a wordCounter that counts CJK characters individually, and derive reading time from the text using the same word/CJK split and rates (200 words/min, 500 CJK chars/min) as the published reading-time util, so the editor and the rendered page agree.

entries.title: isAlphanumeric el-GR range omits accented Greek that isAlpha accepts
entries.symptom: isAlpha('el-GR') uses the Greek range [Α-ώ] (U+0391–U+03CE) but isAlphanumeric('el-GR') still ends at ω ([0-9Α-ω], U+03C9). So isAlphanumeric rejects ό, ύ, ώ and the uppercase Ό/Ύ/Ώ even though isAlpha accepts them, and common words like νερό or πρώτα pass isAlpha but fail isAlphanumeric.
entries.repro:
isAlpha('νερό', 'el-GR')        // true
isAlphanumeric('νερό', 'el-GR') // false  <- ό (U+03CC) sits past the range end ω
entries.fix: Bump the alphanumeric Greek range to [0-9Α-ώ] so it matches the alpha class.

entries.title: Galician formats June as xuño but cannot parse it back
entries.symptom: In the gl (Galician) locale the June parse pattern is /^xun/i. It matches the abbreviation "xun" but not the wide form "xuño", because the third character is ñ, not n. So format then parse round-trips fail for June; the locale's own snapshot already records Invalid Date for June while the other eleven months parse.
entries.repro:
const s = format(new Date(2021, 5, 1), 'MMMM', { locale: gl }); // 'xuño'
parse(s, 'MMMM', new Date(), { locale: gl });               // Invalid Date
entries.fix: Widen the June pattern to /^xu[nñ]/i so it matches both "xun" and "xuño" while staying distinct from July ("xul"), mirroring locales such as Catalan that already fold diacritics into their patterns (e.g. /^març/i).

entries.title: CsvParseStream leaves a leading BOM glued to the first header key
entries.symptom: The synchronous parse() strips a leading UTF-8 byte-order mark (U+FEFF) but CsvParseStream does not. When a CSV begins with a BOM, common output from Excel and other Windows tools, the first field name arrives as "\uFEFFname" instead of "name", silently corrupting header-based lookups.
entries.fix: Strip the BOM from the first line read by StreamLineReader, matching what parse() already does via its BYTE_ORDER_MARK constant.
